title: cordylanthus rigidus var. brevibracteatus
intro: Cordylanthus rigidus var. brevibracteatus, also known as the bracted bird's beak or bird's beak, is a rare and endangered plant species native to California in the United States. It belongs to the family Orobanchaceae and is a hemiparasitic plant, meaning it obtains some of its nutrients by attaching to the roots of other plants. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate various health issues such as colds, coughs, and skin irritations. Cordylanthus rigidus var. brevibracteatus can be prepared as a tea or tincture to harness its health benefits, and it has been traditionally used in herbal remedies for its ability to soothe sore throats and calm digestive issues.
